content.body:
I think it is pretty awesome that you are continuing to support the work of the Browser Standards littledan , even after leaving so abruptly. I have a couple comments about your slides:

1. The fact that you named me and suggested that I wrote "almost all" of the standard is not a fair characterization. I designed the approach and wil take the fall for that, but the way you state it is unfair to the contributions of the others, and shows that you don't understand the harmonized standards: It is not my name that will be signed at the end of the process. Also, do you have the explicit permission of Andrew to name him in your screenshot? If so, ok - but just calling that out.
2. The final slide is, I am assuming, leftover from FOSDEM. Please revise it. ETSI's policy on submissions is something you are currently mischaracterizing. You are also not the point of contact anymore, that is why I am sure it is mere oversight.

Also, do you have the explicit permission of Andrew to name him in your screenshot? If so, ok - but just calling that out.
  2. The final slide is, I am assuming, leftover from FOSDEM. Please revise it. ETSI's policy on submissions is something you are currently mischaracterizing. You are also not the point of contact anymore, that is why I am sure it is mere oversight.

content.body: How do the slides mischaracterize the submission policy?
